item.title: Bit chilly innit?? [OC]
item.description:
Every darn day! I can’t wait for summer :,D good thing I love snuggling in warm jumpers and thick fluffy blankets!
Of course the mighty Chonker cat tax! If you want to support my comics do check out my Patreon! https://www.patreon.com/AmyRevives
item.dc:creator: AmyRevives

item.title: Crimson Dump
item.dc:creator: derek237

item.title: What Happened Today #76
item.description: They’re dismantling the system from every angle, and this week, they escalated. The IRS is being weaponized, science boards are purged, disaster programs axed, and even Medicare data is being turned into a surveillance tool. But resistance is biting back: a judge threatens contempt over illegal deportations, Schumer blocks Trump’s DOJ picks, and California sues over economic sabotage. While the headlines scream, they’re quietly bleeding public service: cutting AmeriCorps, slashing species protections, and killing off foreign disinfo teams. Don’t just watch the fire. Go fight the arson. Speak out, show up, and stop the sabotage. Subscribe free at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/boasberg-contempt 2. https://go.govbrief.today/vanhollen-salvador 3. https://go.govbrief.today/bondi-abregogarcia 4. https://go.govbrief.today/doge-americorps 5. https://go.govbrief.today/irs-directfile 6. https://go.govbrief.today/labor-doge 7. https://go.govbrief.today/ezkZdoge-verainst 8. https://go.govbrief.today/dod-library-books 9. https://go.govbrief.today/california-tariffs 10. https://go.govbrief.today/hhs-budget 11. https://go.govbrief.today/ny-wind 12. https://go.govbrief.today/pentagon-cirlce-widens 13. https://go.govbrief.today/harvard-taxexempt 14. https://go.govbrief.today/dhs-harvard 15. https://go.govbrief.today/medicare-immigration 16. https://go.govbrief.today/enangered-species 17. https://go.govbrief.today/women-minorities-science 18. https://go.govbrief.today/disinformation-office 19. https://go.govbrief.today/schumer-block

item.title: Reality in Ukraine: While recording a message to the residents of Kherson, the head of the City Military Administration, Roman Mrochko, came under enemy fire. He just stood up and continued his address.

item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: It's been quite a day.
item.description: Seven years ago he suffered a fairly serious head injury. He was hospitalised but somehow the doctors didn't know he was on blood thinnners so the medication they gave him caused catastrophic brain bleeds so he was put into a coma to recover. After a couple of months the doctors told us it was time to prepare to pull the plug but then he rallied and emerged from the coma. He went to rehab and re-learned to walk. The rehabilitation hospital were pressuring us to take him home but home wasn't remotely suitable for someone on a walking frame. We did our best at our expense to adapt the house but we were waiting for a grant to get some rails put around the house and get a shower he could use. Eventually they threatened to just straight up discharge him unless we tried to take him home so we took him for a weekend and he ended up falling and cracking his head on the floor tiles so back to hospital and rehab again. From there he ended up in a dementia ward because the injury caused symptoms close enough to dementia symptoms. But he was doing alright. He kind of hated it but he made the best of it. Three years later my mother started to exhibit the signs of early onset dementia that he could have told us about had he not suffered repeated head injuries. The stress of all this accelerated it. She was told she'd have six months before she needed to enter a home but she made it to well over a year. She passed a year and a half ago, gasping for breath, surrounded by family after accidentally aspirating some food. Dad made it to her funeral. It was probably the most lucid and aware I'd seen him in a few years so it straight up broke my heart that he had to be so at the funeral of the woman he'd loved and cherished for almost fifty years. For a month or two after, when we visited he was brighter and sharper than he'd been for years before but slowly that began to fade. Every visit for the last fourteen months he was either asleep in bed or asleep in a chair. Sometimes his eyes we open, sometimes not. It made little difference. He was put on palliative care fo the third time last weekend. On Monday afternoon we were told we should come in for a visit. He had stopped eating or drinking properly a week before and he had a chest infection. They'd stopped antibiotics for it because he wouldn't swallow and they knew it was time. He had good colour in his cheeks, an oxygen mask on his face, an O2 stat of 90 but otherwise doing well. At 7:50 this morning I got a call to say he had passed and I was glad. The line between care and torture is a fine one sometimes. He wasn't a great dad but he was a phenomenal grandad. He expressed regret (to my wife, never to me) that he had spent so much time working instead of hanging out with us and that he had been a harsh disciplinarian. With my own kids he was everything I wished he could have been with me and I don't begrudge any of them that experience. They got an amazing grandad and he got to be the person he wanted and needed to be. So he's gone, and I'm glad. His suffering is over. Maybe my whole atheism thing is wrong and he gets to go be with his wife and the family that came before him and now he waits for me and mine or maybe I'm right and his suffering is over his matter rejoins the cosmos in some small way. Regardless, there were better dads, there were worse dads but my dad tried his best and in the end, maybe that's all that matters. (the obligatory edit: There are too many replies for me to respond to and we're obviously a bit on the busy side right now but I want you all to know I read and appreciated every single one of them. You're a lovely bunch Imgur.)
item.dc:creator: diemajorthrilldie

item.title: Oh hello big huge guy and other guy, I am 5’7” woman here to solve your behaviour issues.

item.title: I’m back illustrating too! [OC]
item.description:
I’m back illustrating as well as making my comics!
For cat tax I offer you, Ms. Bean in all her kitty glory! If you want to support my comics do check out my Patreon! https://www.patreon.com/AmyRevives
item.dc:creator: AmyRevives
